title: Graph Neural Networks — Message Passing
summary: Operate on graph-structured data (nodes + edges). Each node aggregates information from neighbors via message passing. $L$ layers = $L$-hop neighborhood. The graph structure IS the inductive bias — no spatial locality, no sequence order, just connectivity.
key_insights: Input: node features $h_v^{(0)}$ + adjacency structure $\mathcal{N}(v)$. Output: updated node features $h_v^{(L)}$.
$L$ layers of message passing = each node sees its $L$-hop neighborhood. Like CNN receptive field growth, but on graphs.
Key difference from transformers: GNNs respect graph structure (only neighbors communicate). Transformer = GNN on FULLY CONNECTED graph.
Graph pooling: aggregate all node features → graph-level representation. Like Global Average Pool in CNNs.
Applications: molecular property prediction, social networks, traffic, chip design, knowledge graphs.

mathematical_core.gcn: $$H^{(l+1)} = \sigma(\tilde{D}^{-1/2} \tilde{A} \tilde{D}^{-1/2} H^{(l)} W^{(l)})$$ where $\tilde{A} = A + I$ (add self-loops), $\tilde{D}$ = degree matrix
mathematical_core.aggregation: $\bigoplus$ can be: sum, mean, max. Choice affects what the model can distinguish.

derivations.title: GCN as spectral convolution on graphs

steps.name: Graph Laplacian
steps.math: $L = D - A$. Eigendecomposition: $L = U \Lambda U^\top$. Eigenvectors = graph Fourier basis.

steps.name: Spectral convolution
steps.math: Full spectral: $g_\theta * x = U g_\theta(\Lambda) U^\top x$. Cost: $O(N^2)$ and eigendecomposition.

steps.name: GCN approximation
steps.math: ChebNet: approximate $g_\theta(\Lambda)$ with Chebyshev polynomials. GCN: first-order approximation → $\tilde{A} H W$. Local, efficient, no eigendecomposition needed.

derivations.consequence: GCN is a computationally efficient approximation to spectral graph convolution. Like how CNN kernels approximate spatial Fourier filters.

derivations.title: Transformer as GNN on fully connected graph

steps.name: GNN on complete graph
steps.math: $\mathcal{N}(v) = \text{all nodes}$. Every node aggregates from every other node.

steps.name: Attention as edge weights
steps.math: Self-attention computes edge weights $\alpha_{ij}$ dynamically: $\alpha_{ij} = \text{softmax}(Q_i K_j^\top / \sqrt{d_k})$. These ARE the message weights.

derivations.consequence: Transformer = GNN with learned, dynamic, fully-connected edges. GNNs = transformers with sparse, fixed edges (from data).

related: TRANSFORMER.SELF_ATTENTION — transformer = GNN on complete graph
CNN.CONVOLUTION — spatial convolution = message passing on grid graph

notes: Directly relevant to Anoop's MCP + knowledge graph interests: graph-structured AI tools where nodes represent system components.
